title: Committed review does not treat PR-summary or browser-link results as authoritative scope

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark that committed review distinguishes summary-only GitHub-backed results from the authoritative PR-files retrieval path and does not fail closed too early for explicit PR invocations.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models the failure mode where summary-only GitHub-backed outputs are mistaken for PR-files retrieval and the review fails closed too early even though the preferred direct non-CLI PR-files API path remains available.

description: A committed-review scenario where the invocation supplies an explicit PR number, one GitHub-backed path returns only PR-summary or issue-style metadata, another returns a browser-link result such as `Open on GitHub.com`, and the preferred non-CLI PR-files API path for that same PR number is still available for retrieving the actual changed-file set. The correct review must not mistake those earlier summary-only results for authoritative PR scope or for exhausted retrieval paths.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models the failure mode where summary-only GitHub-backed outputs are mistaken for PR-files retrieval and the review fails closed too early even though the preferred direct non-CLI PR-files API path remains available.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should treat PR-summary, issue-style, status, or browser-link results as insufficient for PR scope resolution and continue to the preferred direct non-CLI PR-files API path.
The committed review should keep the explicit PR number as authoritative input and continue trying the preferred direct non-CLI PR-files API path instead of failing closed after summary-only results.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not emit the fail-closed PR-scope message while only summary-style or browser-link GitHub-backed outputs have been seen and another allowed non-CLI PR-files retrieval path remains available.

notes: Use this case to benchmark that committed review distinguishes summary-only GitHub-backed results from the authoritative PR-files retrieval path and does not fail closed too early for explicit PR invocations.
